23 May 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • U.S. Blockade of Iran Reaches Milestone of Redirecting 100 Ships CENTCOM 23 May 2026 -- U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) forces reached the milestone of redirecting 100 commercial vessels, May 23, while enforcing a maritime blockade against Iran.

  • May 23: Progress in talks to end imposed war as IRGC continues to oversee Strait of Hormuz transits Press TV 23 May 2026 -- In Pakistan-mediated talks with the United States, Iran's foreign ministry on Saturday said both sides have edged closer to a 14-point memorandum of understanding that will end the imposed war of aggression on all fronts.
  • May 22: Iran hosts Pakistani army chief, oversees transit of more vessels through Strait of Hormuz Press TV 23 May 2026 -- Pakistan's army chief, Field Marshal Asim Munir, arrived in Tehran on Friday as part of efforts to end the US-Israeli war of aggression against Iran, as the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) oversaw another 35 ship transits through the Strait of Hormuz.
  • IRGC Navy says 25 ships passed through Strait of Hormuz in 24 hours Press TV 23 May 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) Navy has announced that 25 vessels, including oil tankers, container ships, and other commercial vessels, transited the Strait of Hormuz over the past 24 hours after obtaining permission and under the security coordination of the IRGC Navy.

  • Trump Says Even Odds Of 'Good' Iran Deal Or Renewed Strikes RFE/RL 23 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said on May 23 there were even odds that Washington would secure a "good" deal with Iran or renew its bombing campaign targeting the country.
  • Trump weights military options amid uncertainty over Iran talks People's Daily 23 May 2026 -- The Trump administration is preparing for a fresh round of military strikes against Iran despite ongoing diplomatic efforts, U.S. media reported Friday, citing sources with direct knowledge of the planning.
  • Republican Senator Urges Trump To Let Military 'Finish The Job' In Iran RFE/RL 23 May 2026 -- US Republican Senator Roger Wicker, who has backed President Donald Trump's decision to go to war with Iran, urged Trump to allow the US military to finish what it started with its air campaign against the Islamic republic.

  • Iran, US Moved Closer Toward Resolving Differences - Foreign Ministry Sputnik 23 May 2026 -- Iran and the United States moved closer toward finding ways to resolve their differences, Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Baghaei said on Saturday.
  • Positive step in talks to end imposed war possible if other side shows commitment: Envoy Press TV 23 May 2026 -- Iran's ambassador to Pakistan says a positive step concerning Islamabad-mediated talks between the Islamic Republic and the United States is possible if the other side displays sufficient commitment.
  • Iran, US moving closer to 'finalizing memorandum of understanding' to end imposed war: Baghaei Press TV 23 May 2026 -- Iran and the United States have edged closer to finalizing a 14-point memorandum to end the imposed war, halt American maritime aggression, and secure the release of Iran's blocked assets, the Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman says.
  • Pezeshkian: Iran seeks its legitimate rights in US talks, but past experiences demand caution Press TV 23 May 2026 -- Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian says the Islamic Republic seeks only to restore its legitimate rights in talks with the United States, but that past experiences of negotiating with Americans have led Tehran to proceed with extreme caution.
  • Iran ready for historic fight against any new aggression: Sr. army commander Press TV 23 May 2026 -- A senior Iranian Army commander says the force is ready for a historic fight against any new aggression on the country amid threats from the United States and the Israeli regime that they might launch a fresh assault on Iran.
  • Iran has no trust in US, will never back down from its rights: Qalibaf tells Munir Press TV 23 May 2026 -- Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf says Tehran has no trust in the United States and will never retreat from its national rights in talks with Washington aimed at ending the war in the region.
  • Failure to respect Iran's rights will lead to heavier defeats for Trump: Defense Ministry spox Press TV 23 May 2026 -- Iran's Defense Ministry says US President Donald Trump is sunk in the quagmire of an unprovoked war of aggression against Iran, stressing that he has no choice but to comply with the nation's demands and respect its rights.
  • Iran's UN envoy rejects US allegations about drone attack on UAE nuclear plant Press TV 23 May 2026 -- Iran's ambassador to the United Nations has dismissed US allegations regarding a drone strike against the Barakah nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates, saying Washington abused the Security Council to spread false accusations against Iran and its peaceful nuclear program.
  • Iran FM discusses regional issues, US talks with counterparts Press TV 23 May 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has held separate telephone conversations with his counterparts from Iraq, Turkey, and Qatar to discuss bilateral issues and the latest regional and international developments, including the Pakistan-mediated talks with the United States.

  • Pakistan says Tehran talks made 'encouraging progress' toward final US-Iran understanding Press TV 23 May 2026 -- Pakistan's military says high-level talks in Tehran have produced "encouraging progress" toward a final understanding between Iran and the United States.
  • Pakistani Army Chief Wraps Tehran Talks As Fragile US-Iran Diplomacy Intensifies RFE/RL 23 May 2026 -- Pakistan's army chief, Field Marshal Asim Munir, held a fresh round of meetings with senior Iranian officials in Tehran on May 23 as part of a widening diplomatic push involving Pakistan, Qatar, Gulf states, and Western countries amid growing fears that the conflict in Iran could reignite.
  • Pakistani Army Chie meets Araghchi in Tehran to advance diplomatic initiatives Press TV 23 May 2026 -- Chief of the Pakistani Army Field Marshal Asim Munir, who has traveled to Tehran for consultations with Iranian officials, met Friday with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi to advance diplomatic initiatives.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Rubio's Stark Warnings To NATO On Russia, Troops, And Alliance Strains RFE/RL 23 May 2026 -- HELSINGBORG, Sweden -- US Secretary of State Marco Rubio ended a tense round of NATO talks in Sweden with a blunt message to European allies: Washington's military footprint on the continent will shrink over time, but the US insists its commitment to NATO's collective defense remains intact.

  • Trump soon to decide on Taiwan arms sales: Source CNA 23 May 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump will decide on Taiwan arms sales soon, a source familiar with the matter told CNA on Friday, after media reports said Washington had paused a proposed US$14 billion arms package for Taiwan because of the war with Iran.

  • A nation that manufactures its own weaponry writes its own destiny: Raksha Mantri India PIB 23 May 2026 -- "A nation that manufactures its own weaponry writes its own destiny," said Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh as he, along with Maharashtra Chief Minister Shri Devendra Fadnavis, inaugurated a Defence Manufacturing Complex of a private sector company, NIBE Group' at Shirdi on May 23, 2026. The complex intends to manufacture advanced artillery systems, missile & space technologies, rocket systems, energetic materials, and autonomous defence platforms.

  • Review of landmark nuclear treaty breaks up without consensus, raising arms race fears UN News 23 May 2026 -- After four weeks of negotiations at UN Headquarters in New York, the 11th Review Conference of the nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT) ended on Friday without consensus on a final declaration.
  • UN chief 'disappointed' after nuclear treaty conference fails to reach agreement Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 23 May 2026 -- UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres on Friday expressed disappointment after a major UN conference on preventing the spread of nuclear weapons ended without an agreement among participating countries., according to Anadolu Agency.
  • Iran says Western states responsible for failure of NPT conference Press TV 23 May 2026 -- Iran says illogical stances adopted by the United States and its allies were responsible for the failure of a major United Nations Review Conference of the Parties to the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons, held in New York earlier this week.
  • Foreign Ministry statement on the ending of the NPT Review Conference Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 23 May 2026
  • Statement on the 2026 Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty Review Conference Australia DFAT 23 May 2026 -- "The Australian Government is disappointed that the 11th Review Conference of the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (NPT) did not reach a consensus outcome."

  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio with Vishu Som of NDTV US Dept. of State 23 May 2026
  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio with Siddhant Mishra of CNN-News18 US Dept. of State 23 May 2026
  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio And U.S. Ambassador Sergio Gor Arriving at a Roosevelt House Reception US Dept. of State 23 May 2026

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • Hamas: Israeli attacks in Gaza constitute 'clear violation' of agreements Press TV 23 May 2026 -- The Palestinian resistance movement Hamas says the Israeli regime's ongoing crimes in the Gaza Strip constitute a "clear violation" of agreements and understandings brokered by mediators.

  • Israeli armed forces kill 6 paramedics, 5 civilians in southern Lebanon Press TV 23 May 2026 -- Israeli airstrikes across southern Lebanon have killed at least 11 people, including paramedics, rescuers, and a child, and wounded several others, as the occupying regime continues to violate the ceasefire.

  • Araghchi reaffirms Iran's unflinching support for Hezbollah in message addressed to Sheikh Qassem Press TV 23 May 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has reiterated the Islamic Republic's unwavering support for the Lebanese Hezbollah resistance group, stressing that it is Tehran's pledge to back justice-seeking movements worldwide.

  • France bans Israel's minister Ben-Gvir amid outrage over abuse of Gaza flotilla activists Press TV 23 May 2026 -- France has banned Israeli minister Itamar Ben-Gvir from entering its territory following international outrage over his treatment of activists detained during the occupying regime's raid on the Gaza-bound Global Sumud Flotilla.

    • Other Conflicts

  • UNICEF Deputy Executive Director Ted Chaiban United Nations Security Council Briefing on "Threats to international peace and security." UNICEF 23 May 2026

  • ICC: Duterte to stay detained Philippine News Agency 23 May 2026 -- Former president Rodrigo R. Duterte will stay detained at the International Criminal Court (ICC) in The Hague, the Netherlands, a trial chamber ruled Friday.

News Reports

  • Ebola Response Update - May 23, 2026 US Dept. of State 23 May 2026
  • WHO chief calls for urgent Ebola action and pandemic preparedness UN News 23 May 2026 -- The recent Ebola and hantavirus outbreaks demonstrate that the world is still vulnerable to rapidly spreading infectious diseases, Tedros Ghebreyesus, the head of the World Health Organization (WHO), warned on Saturday at the close of the 79th World Health Assembly in Geneva.

  • 11th Annual BRICS Counter Terrorism Working Group (CTWG) Plenary and Sub Groups' Meetings India MEA 23 May 2026 -- The 11th Annual BRICS Counter Terrorism Working Group (CTWG) Plenary and Sub Groups' Meetings were held in New Delhi on 21-22 May 2026 under the Chairship of India.

  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's remarks at the 34th Assembly of the Council on Foreign and Defence Policy, Moscow, May 23, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 23 May 2026

  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 23 May 2026

  • U.S. Secretary of State calls on Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi India MEA 23 May 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi US Dept. of State 23 May 2026

  • Foreign Minister Tsahkna meeting with Czech President in Prague: Europe has a great deal to learn from Ukraine Estonia MFA 23 May 2026 -- Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna is on an official visit to Prague, during which he met today with Czech President Petr Pavel to discuss European security, support for Ukraine, and the continued isolation of Russia.
